Lieutenant General Abdulla Khalifa Al Marri, Commander in Chief of Dubai Police, visits the Ports Police Station, whose jurisdiction extends along Dubai’s coastline, covering 13 operational areas, including six marine zones and seven coastal sectors, as well as islands and waterways. During the visit, Al Marri reviewed the station’s operational achievements in the maritime field, including securing 51 marine events and 23 marine races, carrying out 11 security campaigns, producing 156 marine safety reports and 53 security reports, responding to 143 maritime incidents, recording 1,313 marine violations, and implementing a range of security and awareness initiatives.

Dubai Customs organises the Strategic Trade Futures Leadership Programme in collaboration with the National University of Singapore, Asia’s top-ranked university, and a number of leading global organisations across the ports, aviation and customs sectors. The programme aims to strengthen leadership readiness for global shifts and reinforce Dubai Customs’ leadership in developing a future-ready customs ecosystem that supports Dubai’s competitiveness and its position as a global hub for trade and logistics. It forms part of Dubai Customs’ drive to develop mid-level and emerging leaders. @DubaiCustoms

Dubai’s Roads and Transport Authority awards the contract for the Latifa bint Hamdan Corridor Development Project. Extending 12 km at a cost of AED 2 billion, the project will form a new strategic corridor that strengthens connectivity between Dubai’s key road corridors. The project includes the construction of seven bridges extending 2,300 metres and eight tunnels covering 900 metres, enhancing traffic flow and improving the efficiency of the road network. @rta_dubai

The IRGC Navy has announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz until further notice and until U.S. interventions cease.
The IRGC said its aerospace forces hit US Prince Hassan airbase in Jordan, targeting infrastructure and MQ-9 drone hangars, after the U.S. attacked coastal bases and telecom towers in southern Iran.

IRGC Commander Major General Ahmad Vahidi emphasized that avenging the martyrs of the US-Israeli attack and delivering justice remains a firm and legitimate demand. He vowed that this demand will persist in the collective memory of the Islamic Ummah and the Axis of Resistance until the perpetrators—especially the US military—are held accountable. He also reaffirmed the IRGC’s commitment to continuing the path of the martyr Leader of the Islamic Revolution under the guidance of Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei.
